title: ENJOYING HEAVENLY PLEASURES | Bhagavad Gita Verses 9.20 - 9.25 | HG Amitasana Das | HKM Mumbai
speaker: HG Amitasana Dasa

segments.text: So, today we will start the discussion on a very, very important topic and it is to
relate with a lot of confusion which people have, more so in India where we see people
worshipping different deities, different devatas and everyone seems to be convinced that my
God is the God.
So, today the session will cover those aspects and also
the
some very important aspects which will make understanding of why we have to practice spiritual
principles and how it is different from good work, Punya Karma.
So, some very important insights we will be sharing based on the teachings of Srila Prabhupada.
We will chant this verse together.
Please repeat after me.
This is the 22nd verse from the 9th chapter.
Today we will be covering verse number 20 to 25.
So, we skipped the 20th verse.
I thought it was little long and difficult to pronounce.

So, the translation for 20th verse is, those who study the Vedas and drink the Soma juice, seeking the heavenly planets, worship me indirectly. They take birth on the planet of Indra, where they enjoy godly delights.
So, the understanding we get from this verse, they are higher planetary systems, which have a different nomenclature. Sometimes people address this as heaven, swargaloka or higher planetary systems.
Just like even in this planet earth, we have countries like Somalia, where the standard of living is higher.

Likewise, in this universe, there are planets where the standard of happiness, the duration of life is much higher, and they are called higher planetary systems or swargaloka, and they are lower planetary systems, where there is more misery and definitely the standard of living.
So, the standard of living is much lower than what we have here in the planetary system.
So, now Vedas recommend different kinds of sacrifices, different kinds of worship, different devis and devatas are mentioned in the Vedas.
Can anyone help me with an answer? Why the Vedas are recommending worship of different devis and devatas?
Indra Yagna Karo, Ad Lakshmi Puja Karo.
Or in this festival, Ye Puja Karo.
Why it is like that? It could have been much simpler, much easier if it was just one God and everyone was worshipping that same one God and there was utter clarity.
There wouldn't be fights in this world, people fighting in the name of religion.
Why the Vedas are recommending worshipping different devis and devatas? Anyone?
Different needs.
Different needs.
Why can not that one supreme God provide all the needs?
Why I have to worship Indra for rains?
Why I have to worship Saraswati for learning?
Why I have to worship Lakshmi for wealth?
Why not same supreme Lord I worship and I get everything?
He has given different departments of...
He could have managed himself.
See, in this company, you know, CEO actually is limited.
He cannot do all the work.
So, he has no option but to delegate.
But our definition of supreme Lord is he is all powerful.
He can manage everything.
But why different devi and devatas are created in the first place, point one.
Point two and Vedas which is coming from the Lord himself.
Krishna says, Vedaishcha Sarvair Aham Eva Vedya.
I am the creator of the Vedas.
Vedaantakrit Chaham.
And in that Vedas, Krishna's recommendation is do Indra Yagna, do Lakshmi Puja, do Saraswati Puja and other devi and devatas.
And thereby, we all have got confused.
In Bangalore, the place where we have our temple.
In a one kilometre radius, there are nine temples of different devi and devatas.
So, for a common man who has to worship someone, it is very confusing.
Where do we go?
So, any answer?
He has deputies. Why he wants deputies?
See, the question what I was asking was, in a company, let's say Bill Gate is running Microsoft.
He needs deputies.
He is a human being.
He has limitations.
He cannot expand himself.
But Lord can do that.
So, we will come to that.
Now, first thing we have to understand.
So, we are covering some very fundamental aspects today.
First thing is, Vedas is a body of knowledge which has come from the Lord.
Tene Brahma Hridaye Adi Kavaye.
This Vedas is not a man-made book or a scripture.
It is Aparushaya.
Aparushaya means it is not man.
Parush means man.
Aparushaya means it is not man-made.
It is coming from the Lord Himself.
Just like any creation, any gadget,
any product in the market is accompanied by a manual by the same manufacturer
which talks about how the product has to be used.
Likewise, for this creation, we have the Vedas
which was revealed at the dawn of creation.
Now, in the Vedas, Vedas are dealing with a huge spectrum of human beings.
There are totally how many types of living entities?
Different species, plants, birds, aquatics, reptiles.
How many of them?
84 lakhs out of which 4 lakhs are human beings.
And these divisions is not basis of color,
not basis of religion, not basis of caste,
not basis of color.
All of these are spiritual evolution.
So different people are at different stages of their spiritual evolution.
Quite possible, we all here are different species.
Although we have many things in common.
We are human beings.
We stay in Mumbai.
We stay in Mumbai.
Many things in common.
There could be different species.
So Vedas have to now address the requirement of this huge spectrum.
Right on one side of the spectrum is complete aborigines,
cannibals, uncultured human beings,
more like animals, almost like animals.
And the other side of the spectrum is completely exalted,
Paramahamsa, pristine, pure.
So these are the different personalities.
Now you cannot have different strokes for different folks.
Have you heard of that?
Now in the school also you have different classrooms.
First standard, second standard, third standard.
Because the level of understanding of different subjects is different of different students.
According to the age group they divide and they teach a particular level of maths
and then graduate to the next level,
then to the next level,
then to the next level.
You don't have a school where students from first standard to tenth standard
sit in the same classroom and the same teacher is starting the same subject.
No.
So likewise the Vedas,
knowing that people are at different levels of their spiritual growth,
just like in a school everyone cannot understand trigonometry and algebra.
There are students who don't even know 2 plus 2 is 4.
So for them,
the way the maths is taught is different.
For a student in the eighth standard,
the way the maths is taught,
the subject is the same,
but the way it is administered and taught is different.
So likewise there are people who are not interested in God.
The bottom end of the spectrum are people who are completely maybe not interested in God.
And yes,
the other side of the spectrum are completely God conscious people,
pure,
self-controlled,
free from false ego,
free from pride,
free from greed,
very pure in their character.
Now,
the Vedas talk about Dharma
and by following Dharmic principles,
Dharmic principles,
we can fulfill our Kama,
our desires.
We will get Artha.
Artha means prosperity,
material,
whatever our requirements gets fulfilled.
And ultimately,
Dharma is meant to take us to Moksha.
So these four lakh species are there,
each of them at different levels of spiritual evolution.
But all in this material world,
the ultimate goal of Vedas is to take them to higher realms of understanding.
And finally,
finally what?
Take them out of this material existence,
which is called Moksha.
In different religions,
it's called by different names.
And ultimate goal is to go back to the kingdom of God,
our Father in heaven.

somebody calls it Jannat,
somebody calls it by different names.
So now people are at different levels.
So,
some are just interested in Artha
and fulfilling their Kama.

They are not interested in God per se.
They are just interested in Artha and Kama,
fulfilling their desires.

at that level of spiritual evolution,
they are just interested in Kama and Artha,
they say that,
if you have to fulfill this Artha and Kama,
worship this.
At least the concept of,
there are some higher personalities
whom I have to approach,
whom I have to surrender to,
whom I have to worship,
is a very very big stepping stone
for a person who is completely oblivious of God.

that he is not God.
For his requirement,
he has to approach some higher power.

they are not interested.

they are not interested.

are interested in Artha and Kama.

knowing that these people are just interested in Artha and Kama,
there are some recommendations for such people that,
you worship this.

You will get prosperity.
So more people are interested in Lakshmi Puja
because they are interested in Lakshmi.

just like Ravana wanted to enjoy,
he was a pious man,
he was a great believer in,
he was a devotee also,
yes or no?

He was not interested in Rama.
So there are recommendations for such people that,
okay,
if you are interested in prosperity,
Artha,
not interested in Moksha,
not interested in God,
fine,
do Lakshmi Puja.
At least they are doing some Puja.
Now here Krishna is saying that,
when we worship any Devi and Devatas,
you are worshipping me only.
You see the verse here.
I think it comes in the next verse,
I will come to that.
But what we are doing is,
worshipping me only,
Krishna is saying,
and it is indirect worship,
avidhi purvakam.
na me vidhu sura ganaha prabhavam na mahar shaya aham adir hi deva naam

aham adir hi deva naam.

mahar shi naam sa sarva shaha

and we have different Devi and Devatas.

in the 9th chapter

We have different incarnations,
we have different expansions
and
it is recommended that you worship different Devi and Devatas.
When they have thus enjoyed heavenly pleasure,
by worshipping different Devi and Devatas,
our karma can get fulfilled.
Our requirement for Artha can get fulfilled.
We can achieve heavenly happiness.
We can even go to Svargaloka,
the planets of the Devatas,

here it is said,
when they have thus enjoyed heavenly sense pleasure,
we have got Artha
and our karma is fulfilled,
they return to this mortal planet again.
Thus though the Vedic principles,
they achieve only flickering happiness.
Temporary happiness.

it is very, very prosperous, economically developed, still janma, mrityu, jara, vyadi
is still there. Just two days back, Barbara Bush, she was 92 years, wife of a president
and mother of another president in America. And not an ordinary American, wife of the
president. She is not free from mrityu. She is not free from old age. I will show you

treatment, anti-aging creams. So just like we see in this world, you have places which
are very prosperous but still jara, vyadi, jara, vyadi, jara, vyadi, jara, vyadi, jara,
vyadi, jara, vyadi, jara, vyadi, jara, vyadi. Here we are not free from janma, mrityu, jara
and vyadi. Steve Jobs, Apple. Everyone looks forward to working with Apple. He is a CEO.

not help. So here we have an example. One could be
very famous.
could be very rich, could be very prosperous, could be living in the best of the world, best of countries but still he is not absolved of, she is not free of Janma, Mrityu, Jara, Yati.
Likewise, by accumulating Punya, by doing lot of Dharma, all our Artha requirements and Kama requirements can be fulfilled in this material world
and we cannot get freedom from the cycle of birth and death.
Punarapi Janma, Punarapi Bharat.
Barbara Bush, she was the President wife, may be the wife of the President of United States, what happens to the soul now, don't know.
So by worshipping different Devatas, our Artha requirement gets fulfilled.
Our Kama can get fulfilled but we will not get Moksha.
So, Shrutha, Shrutha means education, intelligence.
All of us are, you see I was just reading an article, Mark Zuckerberg when he was called for the Congress in the United States because of this leaks of data.
You know he is controlling the data of billions of people.
What kind of brain he must be having.
Frankly speaking, we are actually struggling to maintain database of 150 people.
Who came, when didn't come, many times we end up making a mistake, although we capture the data.
Billions of people, what time he is logged in, what time he is logged out, what kind of post he has shared, everything is being captured and analyzed.
What kind of brain that person may be having.
Who can think that how billions of people the data, the post is being, you know the servers are maintaining the data, the archival of that data.
He is a young man.
So here we see people who are very very intelligent, brilliant and some people you know, Buddha.
Did we choose? No.
Yes, we can sharpen our memory, sharpen our brain, some exercises, but then yes we are blessed with Janma Aishwarya Shrutashri.
So by worshipping, now here it's like a fairy wheel.
We go up and we go down.
One life in a heavenly planet, next life, vena sheene punya marte lokam vishanti.
When our Punya gets exhausted, we come back to this material, to this heavenly planet.
We go to the heavenly planet, exhaust all our Punya and then again come back to this earthly planet.
So getting out of the cycle of birth and death is not possible by worshipping different Devatas.
But still worship of Devatas play a role for a people who are at one level of spiritual evolution.
At least they are learning the understanding that you have to approach higher personalities.
Right now they don't have the understanding of who is the highest.
What is the highest form of worship?
Whom we have to ultimately surrender.
Now this is Lord Shiva saying, mukti pradata sarvesham vishnur eva.
This is Lord Shiva speaking to Parvati.
This is in Padma Purana.
Mukti pradata sarvesham vishnur eva na samshaya.
Samshaya means doubt.
There is no doubt that mukti pradata, to giver of mukti is Vishnu himself.
There is no doubt that Vishnu is the deliverer, liberation for everyone.
They are, in fact, Shiva Purana, it is recommended that you worship Shiva.
Yes, by worshipping Shiva,
all their Ashutoshas, Shiva is also called Ashutoshas because he gets easily pleased.
He gives benedictions very easily.
And that's why people who are more interested only in benedictions, they find it is much better.
So to deliver such people, he has taken the responsibility by worshipping him.
At least they are taking some steps towards their spiritual growth.
Now, ananyas chintayanto maam.
Ye jana pariyupasana, without any deviation.
Ananyas, anya means others.
Ananya, ananyas chintayanto maam.
Who is saying this? Krishna.
Ananyas chintayanto, those who worship me with undivided focus.
Ye jana pariyupasate, who worship me.
Tesham nityabhuktanam yogakshemavahamiham.
Now, we have those who worship me with devotion, meditate on my transcendental form.
To them, I carry what they lack and preserve what they have.
Now, we have nine limbs of bhakti, which is also called navavidha bhakti.
Which is different from worshipping different devatas.
In this navavidha bhakti, the aim, the objective is to please the Supreme Lord, to glorify the Supreme Lord.
And it starts with shravanam.
Shravanam means hearing about the Lord.
Then we have kirtanam, smaranam, padhasevanam, archanam, vandanam, dasyam, sakhyam, atmani vedam.
I will not go into details of each.
There are nine ways in which we can worship and serve the Supreme Lord.
When we start navavidha bhakti, in any one form, could be shravanam, could be kirtanam.
Krishna says, I provide what a person lacks.
And preserve what a person has.
Such activities are all auspicious and full of spiritual potencies.
This chanting of Hare Krishna mantra, what we are doing, it is not to fulfill any of our material desires.
Although we may start with that, but the main benefit of this is, it is propelling us towards the ultimate goal.
To purify our existence, purify our consciousness.
And then eventually go back to the kingdom of God.
The Lord helps the devotee.
If we take one step towards the Lord, the Lord is very very eager to take ten steps towards us.
And it says that the Lord's step is much bigger than our step.
So here the Lord says, I provide what the devotee lacks and preserve what he has.
So now this is the ladder, yoga ladder.
Starts with karma kanda.
You will not be able to see, but nevertheless I will explain.
Karma kanda is where we are more interested in artha and kama.
So there are sections in the Vedas which talks about, you do this, you will get this.
The centre is us.
And then we have sakama karma yoga.
I will not get into details now, it is a separate topic by itself.
And then we have nishkama karma yoga.
Third chapter Krishna talks about karma yoga.
Fifth chapter Krishna talks about karma yoga.
In sixth chapter Krishna talks about jnana yoga.
And then we have understanding of ashtanga yoga.
And finally in the ninth chapter Krishna is talking about bhakti yoga.
Ananyas cintayanto maam.
Which involves not any kama, not any desire for artha.
It is mainly to serve the Supreme Lord.
Hear about the Lord, chant the glories of the Lord, serve the Lord, worship the Lord.
And these activities will free us from this cycle of birth and death.
